nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: The root application component that manages user authentication state, fetches initial logs/data for administrators, and routes users to either the admin dashboard, the driver portal, or login views.

nodes.name: AnalyticsView.tsx
nodes.summary: Displays visual business intelligence graphs, KPI metrics, route progress statistics, and driver utilization charts using Recharts.

nodes.name: DashboardView.tsx
nodes.summary: Serves as the primary operational workspace for administrators, listing overall shipment indicators, recent actions, and integrating the live ControlCenter maps.

nodes.name: DriverPortal.tsx
nodes.summary: Provides the dedicated interface for drivers, allowing them to view active assignments, update shipment dispatch statuses, record reached checkpoints, and sign out.

nodes.name: FleetView.tsx
nodes.summary: Implements the fleet administration panel, displaying driver counts, availability statuses, and active route progress cards in a clear tabular grid.

nodes.name: Landing.tsx
nodes.summary: The unauthenticated landing page of the application, showing product overview, features, pricing links, and quick-access login/register triggers.

nodes.name: Login.tsx
nodes.summary: Standard login/register form component that communicates with authentication services to request tokens and updates the global user session state.

nodes.name: MetricsGrid.tsx
nodes.summary: A lightweight layout component showing administrative status metrics (Total Shipments, Active Shipments, Driver Utilization, and On-Time Rate) via API integration.

nodes.name: Sidebar.tsx
nodes.summary: Implements the left sidebar navigation layout for the admin view, letting administrators toggle between the main operational views and log out.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Application configuration module that exports REST API and WebSocket endpoint URLs determined by environment variables and local dev states.

nodes.name: authSlice.ts
nodes.summary: Redux slice that manages the authenticated user session, loading states, error responses, and session validation thunks.

nodes.name: hooks.ts
nodes.summary: Provides strongly-typed React Redux hook wrappers `useAppDispatch` and `useAppSelector` for accessing the global store.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Root application component that initializes the app by validating existing session cookies, configuring real-time web sockets for admin roles, and navigating drivers to their portal.

nodes.name: AnalyticsView
nodes.summary: Aggregates driver statistics, delivery indicators, and routes progress, using Recharts components to render status breakdowns, route speeds, and fleet utilization.

nodes.name: DashboardView
nodes.summary: Renders the primary administrator cockpit, displaying real-time metrics cards, shipment lists, active shipment donut slices, and the interactive dispatch control center.

nodes.name: StandbyPanel
nodes.summary: A sub-component rendering a clean standby status layout for drivers who currently have no active shipments assigned to them.

nodes.name: ActiveShipmentPanel
nodes.summary: Displays detailed interactive information about the driver's active dispatch including tracking numbers, origin/destination, target dispatch times, and checkpoints checklist.

nodes.name: DriverPortal
nodes.summary: Main view wrapper for drivers to load and list their assigned shipments, trigger dispatch starts, mark checkpoints reached, and monitor their performance stats.

nodes.name: FleetView
nodes.summary: Renders cards summarizing fleet statistics alongside a tabular layout of all registered drivers, active jobs, and warehouse assignments.

nodes.name: Landing
nodes.summary: Renders a modern landing page for logitrack with styled gradient cards and links leading to login or registration pages.

nodes.name: Login
nodes.summary: Renders a form supporting registration and login using state hooks to handle inputs, trigger post requests to api boundaries, and dispatch Redux actions.

nodes.name: MetricsGrid
nodes.summary: Fills in a grid of cards using metrics endpoints, refreshing details automatically on changes to shipments and drivers state.

nodes.name: Sidebar
nodes.summary: Displays the logitrack brand logo and allows navigation across Command Center, Analytics, Tracking, and Fleet views, as well as signing out.

nodes.name: checkSession
nodes.summary: An async Redux thunk that queries the authentication endpoint to verify session validity and loads user details upon fulfillment.

nodes.name: useAppDispatch
nodes.summary: A custom React Redux hook wrapper that exposes a strongly-typed dispatch function for updating the Redux state.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow configured to run backend tests (with a PostgreSQL service container) and build the frontend application upon push or pull requests to the main branch.

nodes.name: ua-import-map-input.json
nodes.summary: Temporary configuration file containing the input list of workspace files for resolving import mappings.

nodes.name: ua-import-map-output.json
nodes.summary: Temporary configuration file containing the resolved import maps and metrics for the workspace files.

nodes.name: ua-scan-files.json
nodes.summary: Temporary configuration file storing the complete list of files identified during workspace scanning, including their languages and categories.

properties.description: Resolved import map generated from the input file listing.
Input mapping derived from the scanned files listing.

layers.name: Infrastructure & CI/CD
layers.description: Docker files, Docker Compose, Nginx server configurations, CI/CD pipelines, and cloud platform configurations.

layers.name: Configuration & Metadata
layers.description: Project configurations, dependencies manifests, tooling rules, build parameters, environments, and general project documentation.

layers.name: Testing Layer
layers.description: Unit and integration tests, testing environment setup, test helpers, and runner configuration files.

layers.name: Frontend UI Layer
layers.description: React user interface components, views, main application shell, entry point, styling files, and structural HTML templates.

layers.name: Frontend State & Data
layers.description: Redux store configuration, slices, typed state hooks, and web socket handling middleware.

layers.name: Backend API & Core Services
layers.description: Express HTTP entry points, router setups, controller endpoints, and real-time simulator logic.

layers.name: Backend Middleware
layers.description: Express handlers executing before route handlers, such as authorization and token verification.

layers.name: Data Layer & Migrations
layers.description: Database connection clients, seed scripts, Prisma schema, migration locks, and specific SQL table schema layouts.
